title: CR-2026-0142-Core-Switch-Firmware-Upgrade
#Replace7: Upgrade datacentre core switch firmware to the latest supported release
#Replace8: Current firmware approaches end-of-support (2026-09-30). The new release contains critical bug fixes affecting routing convergence and resolves a known core-fabric issue that has driven two P1 incidents in the past quarter. Upgrade is required before EOS to retain vendor support.
#Replace9: Phased upgrade across both core switches during scheduled maintenance windows. Primary core switch on 2026-06-07 02:00-04:00. Secondary core switch on 2026-06-14 02:00-04:00. In-service upgrade mechanism used to minimise traffic impact. Pre-checks include configuration backup, control-plane validation, and a tabletop walkthrough with vendor technical support on standby.
#Replace10: Medium. Transient traffic loss expected during supervisor failover (<200 ms). Brief routing reconvergence may impact long-lived TCP sessions. Pre-change vendor support ticket opened. Rollback supported via dual-image boot configuration.
#Replace11: APPROVED - Network Operations Manager, IT Operations Director. CAB review 2026-05-30.
#Replace14: Boot to previous image via the documented 'boot system' rollback configuration; estimated rollback time 8 minutes. If routing fails to converge within 5 minutes, escalate to the vendor and revert via console.
#Replace15: Network Operations team, Storage team (downstream SAN traffic), Application Ops on-call, vendor technical support

title: CR-2026-0156-DNS-Provider-Migration
#Replace7: Migrate primary authoritative DNS for corporate domains to a new DNS provider
#Replace8: The new DNS provider offers improved DDoS protection, anycast performance, and consolidates DNS, WAF and CDN management under a single pane. Independent benchmarking shows ~40% faster resolution from European points of presence. Material annual cost savings expected.
#Replace9: Pre-stage all zones in the new provider with TTL reduced to 300s seven days prior. Transfer NS delegation at the registrar during the low-traffic window. Maintain the current provider as authoritative-secondary for 30 days as fallback. Monitor resolution success via internal DNS probes and external synthetic checks.
#Replace10: Medium-High. DNS resolution failure would impact customer-facing services. TTL reduction provides fast rollback if needed. Stakeholder communications planned 14 and 7 days prior.
#Replace11: PENDING - awaiting CAB review 2026-05-30. Pre-approved by VP Infrastructure.
#Replace14: Revert NS delegation at the registrar within 60 minutes if resolution success rate falls below 99.5%. The previous provider zones remain authoritative-secondary for 30 days post-migration.
#Replace15: All customer-facing application teams, Security team (WAF impact), Marketing (corporate site), Customer Support (call-volume monitoring)

title: CR-2026-0163-SAN-Failover-Test
#Replace7: Annual disaster-recovery failover test for the primary SAN platform
#Replace8: Annual DR test required by the IT Disaster Recovery Policy and Internal Audit. Validates documented runbooks, RTO/RPO targets (RTO 30 min, RPO 5 min), and confirms cross-site replication health. The previous test (2025-06) identified two runbook gaps which have since been remediated; this test verifies the fixes.
#Replace9: Controlled failover of tier-2 application volumes from DC1 to DC2 during a planned maintenance window. Validate application-side mount, integrity checks, and reverse failover. Tabletop walkthrough Friday prior; live exercise Saturday with the full team present.
#Replace10: Low. Tier-2 applications only; tier-1 production volumes unaffected. Failback is tested and documented. On-call team on standby with a vendor support ticket pre-opened.
#Replace11: APPROVED - Storage Manager, Application Owners (tier-2 only), Internal Audit (observer).
#Replace14: Reverse failover via documented runbook (RTO 20 minutes). If anomalies are detected during reverse failover, escalate to the storage vendor; the documented manual procedure remains available as a last resort.
#Replace15: Storage team (lead), tier-2 application owners, Database team, Backup team, Internal Audit observer, storage vendor support

title: CR-2026-0171-VPN-Concentrator-Replacement
#Replace7: Replace legacy VPN platform with a new zero-trust remote access platform
#Replace8: The legacy VPN appliances reach EOL 2026-08-31. The new platform provides zero-trust aligned access controls, integrated MFA, posture assessment, and reduces TCO by ~22% over the contract term. Required to meet the revised remote-access Security Architecture Policy.
#Replace9: Parallel-run the new platform with the existing one for 30 days. Phased user migration by department (50 users/week, 8 weeks). Authentication via the corporate identity provider. Decommission the legacy appliances after the final migration cohort and a 14-day clean window.
#Replace10: Medium. Two parallel-stack platforms during transition increase configuration complexity. Detailed routing tested in lab. Mitigation: dedicated bridge during cutover, vendor sales engineer on-call.
#Replace11: APPROVED - CISO, VP Infrastructure, Network Operations Manager.
#Replace12: Phased: 2026-06-15 through 2026-08-09 (eight migration cohorts, weekly Tuesdays 20:00-22:00 BST)
#Replace14: Restore the legacy platform configuration backup; revert the user profile via the endpoint management platform. Per-cohort rollback is documented; full rollback supported until the decommission window starts.
#Replace15: All remote-access users (~400), Security team, Identity team, Service Desk (volume planning), HR (BAU communications)

title: CR-2026-0228-CRM-Major-Release
#Replace7: Adopt the CRM platform major release (Sandbox 2026-04-18, Production 2026-06-13)
#Replace8: The CRM platform ships three major releases annually; adoption is automatic per the vendor contract. The upcoming release includes critical security updates, a new AI-assisted search experience, and improvements to the field-service module our service team uses daily. No-action would be a contractual breach.
#Replace9: Sandbox upgrade applied 2026-04-18; regression testing complete by 2026-06-06 (Test team). Production auto-upgrades 2026-06-13. Post-upgrade smoke tests 2026-06-13 morning by CRM Ops; full hypercare for five working days.
#Replace10: Low-Medium. Two custom platform code classes flagged for deprecated API usage; remediation complete and merged to sandbox. UAT confirmed no regression in field-service flows.
#Replace11: APPROVED - Head of Customer Operations, CRM Steering Group.
#Replace14: The CRM vendor does not support rollback for major releases. Mitigations: thorough sandbox testing, sandbox restore data prepared, vendor account manager engaged for the hypercare window.
#Replace15: Customer Operations (~120 users), Field Service team, BI team (report-impact review), Integrations team (downstream API consumers)

title: CR-2026-0234-Chat-Retention-Policy
#Replace7: Update team chat retention from 90 days to 30 days for non-regulated workspaces
#Replace8: The updated Records Management Policy aligns with revised data-minimisation principles in the corporate Privacy Programme. Reducing retention limits exposure during a data breach and reduces eDiscovery scope. Regulated workspaces (Finance, Legal, HR) retain at 7 years per current obligations.
#Replace9: Policy applied via the compliance platform admin portal. Regulated groups identified by directory security-group membership and excluded explicitly. Communications issued 30, 14, and 7 days prior. Self-service guidance published on the intranet.
#Replace10: Medium. Loss of access to chat history beyond 30 days for ~80% of staff. Mitigation: 30-day comms window, archive-on-demand process available via Service Desk for documented business need.
#Replace11: APPROVED - DPO, Head of Information Governance, CIO.
#Replace12: 2026-06-01 (policy effective; data older than 30 days deleted progressively over the following 14 days)
#Replace14: Policy reversion is possible at any time. However, data deleted under the new policy cannot be recovered. Recommendation: rigorous comms during the deletion window so affected users export history they need.
#Replace15: All team chat users (~3,200), Information Governance, Legal, Finance, HR, Internal Communications

title: CR-2026-0241-HR-ERP-Interface
#Replace7: Deploy a new HR-platform-to-ERP interface for daily employee cost-centre synchronisation
#Replace8: The current manual file-based interface produces 4-6 hour latency in cost-centre updates and has caused three Finance quarter-close adjustments in the past year. A real-time interface enables same-day reconciliation and reduces manual reconciliation effort by an estimated 60 hours per month.
#Replace9: HR platform outbound integration via the corporate integration platform, consuming an ERP inbound channel. Phase 1: read-only sync for verification (two weeks). Phase 2: enable production write. Decommission the legacy file drop after 30 days of stable operation.
#Replace10: Medium. Bad data in the HR platform could now propagate to the ERP within minutes. Mitigation: phase-1 verification, dual-running with the file drop, automated reconciliation reports daily for 30 days.
#Replace11: APPROVED - CFO, CHRO, Head of Enterprise Architecture.
#Replace14: Disable the integration platform flow and restore the legacy file-drop schedule. Verified during the last UAT cycle; estimated revert time 30 minutes. Stale-data exposure window: up to one business day.
#Replace15: Finance team, HR Operations, ERP team, HR platform team, integration platform team, Internal Audit

title: CR-2026-0249-Collaboration-Site-Consolidation
#Replace7: Consolidate 47 legacy collaboration site collections into the new departmental hub structure
#Replace8: Legacy sites (2017-2020) lack consistent governance, contain orphaned permissions, and inflate storage and the search index. The 2026 modern workplace programme requires migration to a hub-based structure. Decommissioning frees 1.8 TB of storage and improves search relevance.
#Replace9: Migration via the chosen migration tool, batched by department (8 batches over 16 weeks). Pre-migration: link assessment, permission audit, user comms. Post-migration: 60-day soft-delete on legacy sites, then hard delete. Search index refresh after each batch.
#Replace10: Medium. Content moves to a new URL pattern; existing links/bookmarks break. The migration tool provides automatic redirects valid for 6 months. Mitigation: per-batch comms, URL redirect map published, "What moved where" portal page.
#Replace11: APPROVED - Head of Modern Workplace, Departmental Heads (consulted).
#Replace14: The migration tool maintains source-site copies during the soft-delete window. Restore by lifting the soft-delete flag (30-minute task per site). Hard-delete after 60 days requires backup restoration (24-hour SLA).
#Replace15: All site owners (~120), Communications, IT Service Desk, migration tool vendor support

title: CR-2026-0314-Emergency-Critical-Vuln-Patch
#Replace7: Deploy emergency patch for a critical CVSS 9.x RCE vulnerability in a core middleware library across all production hosts
#Replace8: The vulnerability (CVSS 9.x) allows unauthenticated remote code execution via a malformed serialisation header. Vendor advisory published 2026-05-22 with proof-of-concept exploit code observed in the wild. Internal SOC confirmed at least one scanning attempt against external surface. Patch deployment is mandated by the Security Vulnerability Policy within 72 hours of vendor release for criticals.
#Replace9: Vendor patch tested in staging on 2026-05-23 morning (functional regression passed). Production rollout in three waves: low-impact services Wave 1 (2026-05-24 evening), medium Wave 2 (2026-05-25 evening), customer-facing Wave 3 (2026-05-26 maintenance window). Each wave preceded by a health-check baseline.
#Replace10: High urgency, medium delivery risk. The patch is fully tested but the production change scope is broad. Mitigation: phased rollout, full team on bridge, vendor support engaged, automated health checks per wave.
#Replace11: APPROVED - CISO under emergency CAB protocol. CIO notified.
#Replace14: Per-host rollback via package-manager downgrade; documented in runbook. Service-level rollback (revert canary): 5-15 minutes per service. The Wave 1 stability gate must pass before Wave 2 proceeds.
#Replace15: All production application teams, SOC, Platform team, Infrastructure on-call, Customer Support (heightened monitoring)

title: CR-2026-0319-Email-Gateway-Egress
#Replace7: Allow outbound HTTPS to the new email gateway SaaS service from the mail-relay subnet
#Replace8: A SaaS email gateway replaces the legacy on-prem appliance. Outbound SMTP from the corporate mail relay needs an explicit allow-list to the vendor public IP ranges. Standard firewall change per the established vendor pattern. Required as part of the parallel email gateway migration change.
#Replace9: Firewall rule added via the network configuration management tool change workflow. Source: mail-relay subnet (10.20.4.0/24). Destination: vendor published egress ranges (auto-updated daily). Port: TCP/443 only. Logging enabled for the first 14 days.
#Replace10: Low. Specific source subnet, well-known vendor destination, standard pattern. Mitigation: rule deployed in monitor-only mode for 24 hours before enforce.
#Replace11: APPROVED - Network Security Lead, Firewall Change Board.
#Replace12: 2026-05-27 Wednesday 14:00 BST (within business hours, low-risk window)
#Replace14: Remove the firewall rule via the network configuration management tool rollback. Estimated revert time: 5 minutes. Mail traffic returns to the existing legacy path during the fallback window.
#Replace15: Email team, Mail-relay platform owners, email gateway vendor support, SOC (logging review)

title: CR-2026-0322-MFA-Contractor-Phase3
#Replace3: D. Roe, Identity & Access Management Lead
#Replace7: Phase 3 of the MFA enforcement programme: extend mandatory MFA to all contractor accounts
#Replace8: Phases 1 (employees) and 2 (privileged accounts) complete since 2025. Phase 3 closes the contractor gap identified by Internal Audit (prior audit finding). Aligns with the revised Authentication Policy and the cyber-insurance renewal requirement.
#Replace9: Enable a conditional access policy targeting the 'Contractors' directory security group. Pre-enrolment campaign with self-service registration portal opens 2026-06-01. Hard enforcement begins 2026-06-15. Service Desk surge resources during weeks 24-26.
#Replace10: Medium. Contractor population (~280 users) is diverse; some may lack compatible devices. Mitigation: device-loan pool of 30 units, alternate-method support (hardware security keys offered), 14-day grace period after enforcement.
#Replace11: APPROVED - CISO, Head of HR (contractor population), Procurement (vendor onboarding paths).
#Replace12: 2026-06-15 enforcement enabled (07:00 BST). Self-service enrolment from 2026-06-01.
#Replace14: Disable the conditional access policy via the IdP admin portal (5-minute task). Documented per the IAM rollback runbook. Contractors revert to single-factor login pending a Phase 3 reattempt.
#Replace15: Contractor population, hiring managers, HR Operations, Procurement, Service Desk, SOC (auth-log monitoring)

title: CR-2026-0331-Internal-PKI-Migration
#Replace7: Migrate internal certificate authority from the on-prem PKI platform to a managed PKI vendor issuing CA
#Replace8: The current PKI infrastructure runs on an end-of-support OS platform (out of support 2027-01). Migrating to a managed issuing CA modernises issuance, automates renewal via the automated certificate enrolment protocol, eliminates a single point of failure, and reduces operational burden. Aligns with the Crypto Modernisation programme (Strategic 2026-2028).
#Replace9: Establish a managed issuing CA chained to the corporate Root CA. Cross-sign for transition. Migrate certificate issuance over 12 weeks by cohort (TLS server, code signing, device, user). Decommission the legacy PKI after a 60-day clean window. Automated enrolment available for all eligible workloads.
#Replace10: High. PKI is foundational - any disruption affects authentication, TLS, code-signing and device-trust. Mitigation: 90-day dual-CA period, comprehensive testing in pre-prod, vendor engineer embedded for the migration period.
#Replace11: PENDING - CAB review 2026-05-30; pre-approved by CISO, CIO, Head of Enterprise Architecture.
#Replace12: 12-week phased migration: 2026-06-22 through 2026-09-14. Decommission window 2026-10-01 through 2026-11-30.
#Replace14: Maintain the legacy PKI in parallel for 90 days; trust both CAs in all clients. Per-cohort rollback by re-issuing from the legacy PKI. Full rollback is documented but increasingly costly after cohort 3.
#Replace15: All platform teams (TLS), Application teams (code signing), Endpoint team (device certificates), IAM team (user certs), SOC, managed PKI vendor support
